violations.text: No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

Raw animal foods (eggs and meat) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (vegetables) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Guidance document previously provided. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

All foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Water was added. Proper sanitizing procedures reviewed with PIC. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

Food packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026
Boxes of food were observed stored on the floor. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the equipment have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. All equipment to be cleaned and sanitized. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026
No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. Expired last year. PIC to renew. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. Application provided. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Dishes were relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (cucumbers) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Dishes placed back in 3-bay. Inspector demonstrated how to set up proper sanitizer. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

Crab Rangoon and egg roll was observed held under 135 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Unit was adjusted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

All foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Spring rolls were was placed hot in the reach-refrigerator in plastic covered containers. Food was placed uncovered in walk-in. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

Food packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
Boxes of food were observed stored on the floor. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the equipment have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. All equipment to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
The 3-bay sink was observed with a large leak. 3-bay to be repaired and maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The floors in the facility has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Whole facility to be cleaned thoroughly. The floor and side of the cash register has a small accumulation of old rodent droppings area to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by new droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2024
A dead mouse was observed on a trap. Mouse to be removed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2024
Allergy statement lacking on menu. Owner added statement at the time of inspection Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/01/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/01/2024

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/01/2024
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/01/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/01/2024
Allergy statement is lacking on the menu. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine and dough maker were observed with an accumulation debris. The ice maker was observed with an accumulation of bio-film. The dough maker was observed with rodent droppings. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC stripped down dough maker at the time of inspection and was in process of sanitizing. Ice was disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

Egg rolls were observed held at 131-134 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Hot hold unit was observed set at exactly 135. Unit was adjusted. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

Employees' personal items (Lotion, deodorant, etc.) were not stored to prevent the contamination of food, equipment, or utensils. Items relocated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Shrimp and chicken was placed hot in the refrigerator in covered containers. Items were unwrapped. Proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

Food packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, allergens, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

The presence of flying insects, rodents is not controlled as evidenced by accumulation of flying insects and rodent droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the slicer have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024
The 3-bay sink was observed with a heavy leak coming from the plumbing. Plumbing to be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024
The door to the kitchen toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 11/12/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

Beef rolls and crab ragoon was observed held at (114-125) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated to 165 degrees fahrenheit Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023

An unapproved bottle of rodenticide spray was observed in the kitchen area. PIC stated he used the spray for fruit flies and now use traps provided by PCS for flies. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout kitchen area and front prep area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment has PCS services 1x monthly. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023
Various food product was observed stored on the floor throughout the establishment. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2023

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of debris: 1.2 door refrigerator and freezer have an accumulation of food residue. 2.food storage carts observed with food residue and debris 3.dry food storage bins and food storage shelves throughout establishment observed with food and soil residue. 4.shelves under 3 bay sink in the front prep area observed with dust, soil residue and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023

The floors throughout the facility and walk in have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023
